stem: A water tank currently contains 200 gallons. Pipe A fills the tank at a rate of 15 gallons per minute, while Pipe B drains the tank at a rate of 5 gallons per minute. Both pipes are open simultaneously. How many minutes will it take for the tank to reach 500 gallons?
explanation: Let t represent the time in minutes. The tank starts with 200 gallons, gains 15t gallons from Pipe A, and loses 5t gallons from Pipe B. The equation is: 200 + 15t - 5t = 500. Simplifying: 200 + 10t = 500. Subtracting 200 from both sides: 10t = 300. Dividing by 10: t = 30 minutes. We can verify: 200 + 15(30) - 5(30) = 200 + 450 - 150 = 500 gallons.

distractorRationale.A: Used only the fill rate without subtracting the drain rate, solving 200 + 15t = 500. = 20
distractorRationale.C: Found the time needed for 300 gallons at just the drain rate instead of net rate. = 60
distractorRationale.D: Incorrectly subtracted rates as 15 - 5 = 3 instead of 10, then solved 200 + 3t = 500. = 100
